title: Make “How to Use” guidance unique per pickup line (remove repeated line text and implement tone detection)

requirements.text: Update the backend `generateHowToUseGuide` so the returned `howToUse` text is actually derived from the pickup line content and does not return the same generic guide for every line.

quotes: they all are still giving the same description

acceptanceCriteria: Calling `getLineWithGuide` for at least 3 noticeably different pickup lines returns guides that differ in their Context and/or Delivery/Timing/Follow-up content (not just the line count).
The backend no longer always classifies every pickup line as the same type when generating the guide.

requirements.text: Implement real `detectLineType` logic in `backend/main.mo` so that it classifies pickup lines into at least several distinct categories (e.g., bold/clever/subtle/playful/cheesy/rhyming/classic) based on simple deterministic heuristics over the text (keywords, punctuation, rhyme-like endings, explicitness, etc.), and use the detected type to select different context/delivery/timing/follow-up guidance.

quotes: they all are still giving the same description

acceptanceCriteria: `detectLineType` no longer returns a constant value (it returns different categories for different inputs).
For each supported category, `generateHowToUseGuide` selects the matching category-specific guidance block (no falling back to the same default for all common cases).
Guide generation remains fully deterministic and uses no external AI/LLM services.

requirements.text: Stop repeating the pickup line text inside the backend-generated `howToUse` guide text (the frontend already displays the pickup line under “The Line”). The `howToUse` string returned by `getLineWithGuide` must contain only guidance content (e.g., Context, How to Use, Timing, Follow-up), not the original pickup line text duplicated at the top.

quotes:
How to Use
Do you like water?

yah...

Then you already like 70 % of me 
Slide me your number and I'll show you what to like about the other 30 %

acceptanceCriteria: In the detail modal, the “How to Use” section no longer starts by repeating the pickup line text verbatim.
The “How to Use” section contains guidance text only and still includes clear English sections (Context, Timing, Follow-up, etc.).

requirements.text: Ensure the generated guide never renders a blank/empty “Context:” section; if no specific context can be inferred, return a non-empty generic context sentence in English.
acceptanceCriteria: `howToUse` never contains a `Context:` label followed by nothing/only whitespace.
If no specific context is detected, a default context sentence is included in English.

constraints: Keep all Motoko logic in the single backend actor in `backend/main.mo` (no additional backend services).
Use English for all user-facing text.
Do not use external AI/LLM services for guide generation.
Do not edit frontend immutable paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
nonGoals: Changing the multi-line validation rules for pickup line submissions.
Adding new backend fields/schema changes for pickup lines (the fix should work by regenerating guides from existing stored text at read time).
Adding third-party integrations for content analysis.
